mission: Make Top-K work explicit across k regimes so agents choose different algorithms and baselines for k=1, tiny k, medium k, large k, and k close to n.
must_not_claim: Do not generalize the existing k<=8 fused sampling seed to medium or large k.
Do not compare sorted custom output against an unsorted baseline without classifying the contract mismatch.
Do not call a custom result a win without same-hardware PyTorch/hipCUB/FlashInfer or relevant serving-stack timing.
Do not hide temporary-storage allocation or workspace reuse in one variant but not another.

custom_candidates: multi-CTA row reduction for few long rows
baselines: hipCUB DeviceReduce/ArgMax-style pair reduction where applicable
serving-stack greedy sampler when decode boundary matters
primary_risks: tie policy mismatch
mask or NaN policy mismatch
launch overhead dominates for small batches

baselines: vLLM on ROCm or vLLM sampler path when serving-facing
primary_risks: register pressure from local candidate arrays
sortedness costs hidden in custom path
hipCUB unsorted output is not deterministic sorted Top-K

custom_candidates: bitonic or pairwise network over candidate tiles
CTA candidate merge with shared-memory tiling
unsorted membership path plus final small sort only when required
baselines: OneFlow heap/full-sort top_k as a framework implementation reference
primary_risks: shared-memory footprint grows as threads*k
network compare count dominates
final sorted repair erases unsorted savings

custom_candidates: partition then local sort of candidates
primary_risks: register spills from large candidate state
temporary candidate buffers dominate memory traffic
threshold repair complexity creates correctness bugs

custom_candidates: full radix sort when k/n is high
baselines: hipCUB DeviceTopK when unsorted output is valid
vLLM persistent_topk for ragged/sparse-attention k=512/1024/2048 indexer contracts
primary_risks: full sort may be simpler and faster for high k/n
workspace allocation dominates if not reused
duplicate keys around pivot break deterministic output

custom_candidates: top-n-minus-bottom selection when k close to n
radix sort with limited key bits
primary_risks: selection algorithm does nearly full-sort work anyway
output write bandwidth dominates
sortedness and stability cost are the operation

baselines: PyTorch per-segment loop or padded topk
hipCUB segmented primitives plus per-segment sort/select
FlashInfer ragged/page-table transforms where contract matches
vLLM persistent sparse-attention Top-K indexer for k=512/1024/2048 when output contract matches
primary_risks: load imbalance dominates arithmetic
padding makes framework baseline unfairly expensive or cheap depending on boundary
empty and shorter-than-k segments need explicit output policy

promotion_rule: A k regime becomes measured only after correctness artifacts plus same-hardware library baselines are attached for at least one representative shape.
